status_legend.in_progress: The feature is the current active task.
status_legend.blocked: Work cannot continue until a documented blocker is resolved.
status_legend.passing: Required verification has passed and evidence is recorded.

features.title: Redefine .gitignore — exclude venv, media, db, IDE files, OS cruft
features.user_visible_behavior: No accidental commits of virtualenv, uploaded media, SQLite database, IDE configs, or macOS artifacts.
verification: Run `git status` in a clean state — only tracked source files appear.
Verify `venv/` is ignored.
Verify `media/` is ignored.
Verify `db.sqlite3` is ignored.
Verify `.DS_Store` is ignored.
Verify IDE dirs (`.vscode/`, `.idea/`) are ignored.
Verify `__pycache__/` and `*.pyc` are ignored.
Verify `.env` is ignored.
evidence: git check-ignore confirms: db.sqlite3, .env, media/reports, venv, .DS_Store all ignored
0 tracked __pycache__ files (removed 150 files from git tracking)
db.sqlite3 removed from tracking (git rm --cached)
./init.sh passes with no errors
Untracked files: only intentional new files (.specs/, AGENTS.md, init.sh)
features.notes: Current .gitignore is minimal. Add standard Python/Django/macOS/IDE entries. Include .env for future env-var support.

features.title: Dockerize — Dockerfile + docker-compose with web and db services
features.user_visible_behavior: Developers can run `docker compose up` and get a fully working VAMPIRE instance with Django and database in isolated containers.
verification: Run `docker compose up --build` from project root.
Verify the Django app starts on http://127.0.0.1:8000/.
Verify the database service starts (PostgreSQL/MariaDB).
Log in and verify data persists across container restarts.
Run `docker compose down` and verify containers are removed.
Run `./init.sh` inside the container works (check + migrate).
Verify media/ files are mounted as a volume (reports survive restarts).
evidence: Dockerfile: python:3.13-slim, apt deps (gcc, libpq-dev), pip deps from requirements.txt, gunicorn
docker-compose.yml: web + db (postgres:16) services, volumes for postgres_data + media_data
.env.example with DB config, SECRET_KEY, DEBUG, ALLOWED_HOSTS template
entrypoint.sh: python manage.py check + migrate + gunicorn startup
.dockerignore: excludes venv, git, pycache, sqlite, media, IDE files
settings.py: DATABASES uses PostgreSQL when DB_HOST env var set, falls back to SQLite
settings.py: SECRET_KEY, DEBUG, ALLOWED_HOSTS read from env vars
requirements.txt: added gunicorn, psycopg2-binary
docker compose up --build: image builds and containers start successfully
docker compose exec web: migration applies all apps, gunicorn starts on 0.0.0.0:8000
Data persistence: finding survives docker compose down/up cycle (PostgreSQL volume)
Media persistence: file in /app/media/ survives docker compose down/up cycle (volume mount)
./init.sh works inside container (check + migrate pass)
Port 8001 used (8000 occupied by Docker Desktop on this machine; configurable in docker-compose.yml)
features.notes: Dockerfile: use python:3.13-slim, install deps from requirements.txt, copy source, run gunicorn. docker-compose.yml: web service + db service (postgres:16 or mariadb:11), volumes for media and db data, .env file for config.

features.title: Database migration — SQLite to PostgreSQL (or MariaDB)
features.user_visible_behavior: No visible change to end users. The application runs on a production-grade database instead of SQLite.
verification: Update settings.py DATABASES to use PostgreSQL.
Run `python manage.py migrate` — all tables created in PostgreSQL.
Verify Django admin works.
Create a project, finding, assignment — verify data is stored.
Generate a report — verify it works.
Run `./init.sh` — passes with PostgreSQL.
Run `python manage.py test` (once tests exist) — passes with PostgreSQL.
evidence: settings.py DATABASES uses PostgreSQL when DB_HOST env var set (with CONN_MAX_AGE=600 connection pooling)
settings.py falls back to SQLite when no DB_HOST configured
.env.example documents both Docker and local PostgreSQL setup with step-by-step instructions
migrate_db.sh: dumpdata from SQLite, loaddata to PostgreSQL with connectivity check
init.sh: warns when using SQLite (recommends PostgreSQL for production)
Integration test on PostgreSQL via Docker: Organization, Project, Assignment, Stakeholder, Finding all created and queried successfully
SPI calculation works on PostgreSQL (SPI=2.01 for test project)
SLA logic works on PostgreSQL (Critical=7d, Medium=30d, late detection correct)
Report generation works on PostgreSQL (PDF generated successfully)
./init.sh passes with SQLite fallback (no PostgreSQL required for local dev)
psycopg2-binary and gunicorn in requirements.txt (added in devops-002)
features.notes: Recommended: PostgreSQL 16 (most Django-community standard). MariaDB 11 is acceptable alternative. Implementation: install psycopg2-binary (PostgreSQL) or mysqlclient (MariaDB), update settings.py DATABASES to read from environment variables (DB_HOST, DB_NAME, DB_USER, DB_PASSWORD, DB_PORT). Keep SQLite as fallback for dev/testing. Add .env.example with DB config template.

features.title: Assessment — apakah project sudah layered architecture?
features.user_visible_behavior: Tidak ada perubahan yang terlihat. Output berupa dokumentasi gap analysis yang mencatat semua business logic yang bercampur di views, models, dan utils.
verification: Audit seluruh views.py — catat semua business logic yang ada di dalam view functions.
Audit seluruh models.py — catat method yang berisi business logic (SLA, SPI, dll).
Audit reports/utils.py dan reports/sections/ — catat logika report generation.
Buat dokumen gap report di .specs/architecture-assessment.md berisi temuan.
Tentukan target structure: services/ per app, repositori pattern jika diperlukan.
Output rekomendasi: apakah perlu refactor penuh atau incremental.
evidence: Full audit of all 10 models.py files: 5 business logic methods found in 2 models (Finding: 4 SLA methods, Project: 1 SPI method)
Full audit of all 11 views.py files: ~30 business logic violations across 6 view files (findings, projects, organizations, reports, vkb, roles)
Full audit of reports/utils.py: generate_report_file() is 60-line monolithic function mixing rendering, I/O, format dispatch
4 apps are clean (assignments, stakeholders, menus, users) with zero business logic in views
Documented 11+ authorization branching duplicates, 4 status lifecycle duplicates, cross-model mutations, raw threading in views
Architecture assessment written to .specs/architecture-assessment.md with gap analysis, target architecture, and 6-phase migration strategy
Recommendation: refactor to layered architecture IS recommended (arch-002), start with Phase 1 (authorization extraction)
Deferred to arch-002: repository pattern is NOT recommended — Django ORM is sufficient data abstraction
features.notes: Saat ini project adalah flat Django FBV: views langsung panggil ORM, business logic tersebar di models (Finding.sla_days, Project.spi), views (create_finding, edit_finding), dan utils (generate_report_file). Tidak ada service layer. Assessment ini adalah prerequisite untuk arch-002.

features.title: Refactor ke layered architecture — services/ per app
features.user_visible_behavior: Tidak ada perubahan yang terlihat oleh user. Internal: business logic dipindahkan ke service layer, views jadi thin HTTP handler.
verification: Buat services/ package di setiap app (findings/services.py, projects/services.py, reports/services.py).
Pindahkan SLA logic dari Finding model ke findings/services.py.
Pindahkan SPI logic dari Project model ke projects/services.py.
Pindahkan report generation logic dari utils.py ke reports/services.py.
Pindahkan finding create/edit lifecycle logic dari views ke services.
Pindahkan project create/update/complete logic dari views ke services.
Refactor views: hanya parse request, panggil service, return response.
Jalankan ./init.sh — system check dan migrate tetap OK.
Manual test: create/edit/delete finding, project, generate report — semua masih berfungsi.
Existing template rendering tidak berubah.
evidence: Created findings/services.py: SlaService (sla_days, sla_due_date, is_late, sla_delay_days) + FindingService (get_queryset_for_user, get_finding_for_user, set_closed_at_by_status, promote_to_vkb)
Created projects/services.py: ProjectMetricsService.spi() + ProjectService (get_queryset_for_user, get_project_for_user, set_completed_date_by_status)
Created organizations/services.py: OrganizationService.get_queryset_for_user()

Created reports/services.py: ReportService (get_mime_type, get_disposition, delete_report_file, file_exists) + ReportGenerationService (create_history, generate_async with try/except, start_generation)
Finding model: 4 SLA methods now delegate to SlaService (backward compatible, templates still work)
Project model: spi() now delegates to ProjectMetricsService (backward compatible)
findings/views.py: all 5 views use FindingService/ProjectService — removed 4 authorization branches, 2 lifecycle duplicates, inline VKB creation
projects/views.py: all 6 views use ProjectService — removed 4 authorization branches, 2 lifecycle duplicates, duplicate @login_required, cleaned up organization_detail
organizations/views.py: organization_list uses OrganizationService — removed authorization branch, cleaned up organization_detail
reports/views.py: generate/download/delete use ReportService/ReportGenerationService — removed raw threading, file I/O, MIME mapping from view. Added try/except error handling in generate_async

Smoke tested: SlaService (7 days Critical, is_late=False), ProjectMetricsService (SPI=2.01), FindingService (close/reopen lifecycle, promote_to_vkb), ProjectService (complete/reopen lifecycle), ReportGenerationService (PDF generation)
./init.sh passes (system check, migrations)
Templates unchanged — backward compatible via model delegate methods
features.notes: Blocked by arch-001 (harus assessment dulu). Target: services/ di tiap app dengan pure functions. Models hanya field + relasi. Views hanya HTTP glue. Import service functions dari views. Tidak perlu repository pattern untuk tahap ini karena Django ORM sudah cukup.

features.title: project_detail returns empty data for assignments, findings, scans
features.user_visible_behavior: When viewing a project detail page, the user sees the list of team members, findings, and scans associated with that project (not empty placeholders).
verification: Create a project with at least 1 assignment, 1 finding.
Navigate to the project detail page.
Verify the assignments list shows the assigned user.
Verify the findings list shows the created finding with title, severity, status.
Verify the reports section shows any generated reports.
evidence: Removed empty assignments: [] context — template accesses project.assignment_set.all directly via ORM
Removed empty findings: [] context — template accesses project.finding_set.all directly via ORM
Removed unused pics: [] context — no reference in template
Kept scans: [] as placeholder for future Scan model feature
Removed duplicate organization_detail dead code (no URL routes to projects/views.organization_detail)
Removed unused imports: get_object_or_404, Organization, ReportHistory
projects/views.py: 116 lines → 64 lines (-45%)
./init.sh passes
Smoke test: project.assignment_set, project.finding_set, project.reports — all return correct data
features.notes: projects/views.py:101 — project_detail view passes empty lists for assignments, pics, scans, findings. And there is a duplicate organization_detail view at line 75 that shadows the one in organizations/views.py. Duplicate @login_required decorator at lines 75-76.

features.title: Report generation thread crashes silently, status stuck 'loading'
features.user_visible_behavior: When report generation fails, the user sees a clear error message instead of a perpetually 'loading' status.
verification: Trigger a report generation for a project.
If generation succeeds, download the file and verify content.
Intentionally cause a failure (e.g. bad template data).
Verify the ReportHistory status is updated to a failure state (not stuck 'loading').
Verify the user sees an error indicator on the project detail page.
evidence: Added "failed" to ReportHistory.STATUS_CHOICES — migration reports/0002
ReportGenerationService.generate_async(): exception handler sets status="failed" before re-raising
Exception still propagates to stderr (visible in terminal) — useful for debugging
project_detail.html: styled status badges — Loading (warning), Done (success), Failed (danger)
Tested: forced exception → status="failed" verified (was stuck "loading" before)
./init.sh passes
features.notes: Partially addressed in arch-002 (generate_async now has try/except). ReportHistory.status needs separate “failed” state and user-visible error indicator on project detail page.

features.title: Write unit tests for models and key views
features.user_visible_behavior: Running `python manage.py test` reports meaningful test results for SLA logic, SPI calculation, and core CRUD views.
verification: Run `python manage.py test`.
Verify tests exist for Finding.sla_days(), Finding.is_late(), Finding.sla_delay_days().
Verify tests exist for Project.spi() with various date scenarios.
Verify at least one view test exists (e.g. project_detail returns 200).
All tests pass with zero failures.
evidence: findings/tests/test_models.py: SlaServiceTest (9 tests) — sla_days with profile, default fallback, due date, is_late open/closed, sla_delay_days
findings/tests/test_models.py: ProjectMetricsTest (4 tests) — spi_on_track, completed_fast, missing_dates, zero_duration
findings/tests/test_views.py: FindingListViewTest (3 tests) — pagination, user access, unassigned project isolation
findings/tests/test_views.py: ProjectDetailViewTest (1 test) — 200 response, contains project name
projects/tests/test_permissions.py: AccessControlTest (2 tests) — involved_only_access, dashboard_redirect
Plus 3 setup_roles tests (8 total from pre-existing)
Total: 22 tests, all pass
init.sh: added [3/3] python manage.py test step
./init.sh passes all 3 steps
features.notes: All tests.py files are empty. Minimum: test SLA logic in findings, SPI in projects, and smoke test for project_detail. Add `python manage.py test` to init.sh once baseline passes.

features.title: Dashboard analytics — severity charts, SPI trends, SLA compliance
features.user_visible_behavior: The dashboard shows interactive charts: finding count by severity, project timeline with SPI indicators, and SLA compliance rate (late vs on-time findings).
verification: Log in and navigate to dashboard.
Verify a chart/graph shows findings grouped by severity (Low/Medium/High/Critical).
Verify a section shows projects with their SPI values and on-time/delayed indicators.
Verify SLA compliance metrics are displayed (e.g. X of Y findings are late).
Verify non-admin users only see data for their assigned projects.
evidence: Dashboard view: computed severity breakdown, SLA compliance %, SPI on-track/delayed counts, recent findings and projects
Dashboard template: 4 stat boxes (Projects, Findings, Late Findings, SLA Compliance %) with color-coded severity
Chart.js doughnut: Findings by Severity (Critical/High/Medium/Low with color mapping)
Chart.js doughnut: Project Schedule SPI (On Track ≥ 1 vs Delayed < 1)
Recent Findings table: title, severity badge, project name
Recent Projects table: project name, status, SPI badge
Non-admin scoping: dashboard only shows data from assigned projects
Template renders 200 with all expected elements
./init.sh passes
features.notes: Current dashboard only shows 2 numbers (project_count, finding_count). Consider Chart.js (already jQuery is loaded) or server-side rendered charts.

features.title: Search bar and advanced filtering on finding list
features.user_visible_behavior: The finding list page has a search bar and dropdown filters. Users can search by title/description and filter by severity, status, project, date range.
verification: Navigate to /findings/.
Type a keyword in the search box, submit.
Verify only matching findings are shown.
Select a severity filter (e.g. Critical), submit.
Verify only Critical findings are shown.
Combine search + filter and verify results.
Verify pagination still works with filters applied.
evidence: finding_list view: GET-param filtering (q, severity, status, project, date_from, date_to)
Search: title__icontains OR description__icontains
Filters: severity dropdown, status dropdown, project dropdown, date range inputs
Pagination links preserve all active filter params via query_string
"Clear" button resets to unfiltered list
Verified: search, single filter, combined filter, empty results — all correct
./init.sh passes
features.notes: Current finding_list view has pagination but no search/filter form. Add GET parameter-based filtering in the view and a filter form in the template.

verification: Navigate to /reports/ — verify a table of reports appears.

Verify filter dropdowns: Project, Format (All/PDF/Word), Status (All/Loading/Done/Failed).
Verify non-admin users only see reports from their assigned projects.
Verify pagination works and preserves filter params.
Verify Preview works for PDF reports.
Verify Download and Delete work.
./init.sh passes.
evidence: reports/views.py: report_list view — select_related project__organization, scoped to user
reports/urls.py: path("") → report_list at /reports/
report_list.html: table with Project (link), Organization, File, Format badge, Status badge, Created, Actions
Filters: Project dropdown, Format dropdown (All/PDF/Word), Status dropdown (All/Loading/Done/Failed)
Pagination: 25 per page, preserves filter params
Non-admin: filtered to assigned projects
./init.sh passes
features.notes: Currently /reports/ 404s — no root URL in reports/urls.py. Add report_list view scoped to user, FilterParams pattern mirroring findings/views.py.

features.title: WYSIWYG editor (Summernote) for PoC field — support image paste/upload
features.user_visible_behavior: When editing a finding, the PoC field shows a rich-text editor with toolbar (bold, italic, tables, code blocks). Users can paste images from clipboard or upload files directly into the PoC content.
verification: Open create/edit finding form — verify PoC field is a WYSIWYG editor with toolbar.
Type text, apply bold/italic/code formatting.
Paste an image from clipboard — verify it appears inline.
Click image upload button — select file, verify it uploads and appears.
Save the finding, view detail page — verify HTML renders as formatted content with images.
Verify XSS is not possible (<script> tags sanitized).
Verify report PDF still renders PoC content correctly.
./init.sh passes.
evidence: django-summernote added to requirements.txt and INSTALLED_APPS
Summernote URL mounted at /summernote/
FindingForm: poc field uses SummernoteWidget (Bootstrap 4 + jQuery compatible)
finding_detail.html: poc rendered with |safe
report PDF template: poc rendered with |safe, removed monospace/pre-wrap styling
./init.sh passes
features.notes: Use django-summernote (Bootstrap 4 + jQuery compatible). Add to INSTALLED_APPS, requirements.txt, and urls.py. poc field stays TextField (content becomes HTML). Templates update poc rendering to |safe. Summernote handles image upload to MEDIA_ROOT automatically.

features.title: Email notification when finding misses SLA deadline
features.user_visible_behavior: When a finding passes its SLA due date, assigned users receive an email notification. A daily digest email summarizes all late findings.
verification: Create a finding with Critical severity (7-day SLA).
Manually set created_at to 8 days ago.
Trigger the notification check.
Verify the assigned user receives an email about the late finding.
Verify the digest includes the finding title, severity, and days overdue.
features.notes: Requires email backend configuration. Can start with console backend (`python -m smtpd`) for development. Consider a Django management command run via cron for periodic checking.

features.title: SLA days configurable per project instead of hardcoded
features.user_visible_behavior: When creating or editing a project, the user can set custom SLA day values per severity level. Findings inherit SLA from their project.
verification: Create a project with custom SLA: Critical=3, High=7.
Create a Critical finding in that project.
Verify the SLA due date is created_at + 3 days (not the default 7).
Create a project without custom SLA.
Verify it uses system defaults (Critical=7, High=14, Medium=30, Low=60).
evidence: SlaProfile model: name, description, sla_critical/high/medium/low, is_default (auto-clears other defaults)
Project model: sla_profile FK (nullable, SET_NULL on delete)
SLA Profile CRUD: sla_profile_list, create, update, delete views + templates
SlaProfileForm + ProjectForm with sla_profile dropdown (empty_label = "Use default SLA")
SlaService.sla_days(): checks project.sla_profile first, falls back to SLA_DEFAULTS (7/14/30/60)
Sidebar: SLA Profiles link under ADMINISTRATION
Project detail: shows SLA profile name + days (or "Default SLA")
Tested: Critical Infra profile → Critical=3d; Standard profile → Critical=5d; no profile → 7d default
./init.sh passes
features.notes: Current SLA values are hardcoded dict in Finding.sla_days(). Move to Project model with default fallback. Consider a global Setting model for system-wide defaults.

features.title: REST API endpoints for findings and projects
features.user_visible_behavior: External tools can query, create, and update findings and projects via JSON REST API with authentication.
verification: Send GET /api/findings/ with auth token.
Verify JSON response contains findings with all fields.
Send POST /api/findings/ with valid payload.
Verify a new finding is created and returned.
Send GET /api/projects/.
Verify response includes project with SPI and finding count.
features.notes: Use Django REST Framework (DRF). Start with read-only endpoints, then add write. Token or session auth. Prioritize findings CRUD and project read.

features.title: Export findings to CSV/Excel, import from scanner output
features.user_visible_behavior: From the findings list, users can click Export to download all visible findings as CSV or Excel. Users can upload a scanner output file (e.g. Burp/Nessus XML) to bulk-import findings.
verification: Navigate to /findings/.
Click Export > CSV.
Verify a CSV file downloads with correct columns (title, severity, status, project, created_at).
Click Export > Excel.
Verify an XLSX file downloads.
Navigate to import page.
Upload a Burp Suite XML file.
Verify findings are created from the import.
features.notes: CSV export: use Django's StreamingHttpResponse with csv module. Excel: use openpyxl or python-docx's excel equivalent. Import: start with a simple JSON/CSV format before tackling scanner-specific parsers.

features.title: Authentication enhancements — password reset, rate-limited login
features.user_visible_behavior: Users can reset their password via email link. Failed login attempts are rate-limited to prevent brute force.
verification: Click 'Forgot password' on login page.
Enter email, submit.
Verify a password reset email is sent (console backend).
Click the reset link, set new password.
Log in with new password.
Attempt 5 failed logins in rapid succession.
Verify the account is temporarily locked.
features.notes: Django has built-in password reset views (django.contrib.auth.urls already included). Just need templates and email config. For rate-limiting: django-axes or manual implementation with django-ratelimit.

features.title: Audit trail — log who created/edited/deleted findings and projects
features.user_visible_behavior: Each finding and project detail page shows a history of changes (who changed what and when). A global audit log page (admin only) shows all recent activity.
verification: Create a finding as user A.
Edit the finding as user B.
View the finding detail page.
Verify an activity section shows: User A created, User B changed severity/title.
Navigate to audit log page as superuser.
Verify all create/edit/delete events are listed with timestamps.
evidence: Created audit app with AuditLog model (content_type + object_id GenericFK, action, user, timestamp, changes)
pre_save/post_save/post_delete signals auto-log create/update/delete for Finding and Project models
pre_save captures old state; post_save compares fields and records changes (e.g. severity: Medium → Critical)
_audit_user attribute on instance identifies the acting user; set in findings/views and projects/views
finding_detail.html: Activity table at bottom showing last 10 audit entries
project_detail.html: Activity table at bottom showing last 10 audit entries
audit/audit_list.html: global audit log (/audit/) paginated (25/page) — admin only
Sidebar: Audit Log link under ADMINISTRATION for superusers
init.sh passes; change detection verified (severity, status, completed_date)
features.notes: Options: django-simple-history, django-auditlog, or a custom AuditLog model with a signal-based approach. Choose the lightest option (custom model likely sufficient).

features.title: Report generation via Markdown — MD as primary output, PDF conversion optional
features.user_visible_behavior: Report generation produces a clean Markdown document. Users can download the Markdown file directly or optionally convert to PDF. Removes the current dual-path (xhtml2pdf + python-docx) complexity.
verification: Generate a report for a project with findings — verify an .md file is produced.
Verify the Markdown contains: cover/title, project info, scope, executive summary, findings table, detailed findings.
Verify the Markdown renders correctly in a viewer (VS Code, GitHub, Typora).
Download the .md file from the project detail page.
Optional: convert the .md to PDF and verify the result.
Verify ./init.sh passes.
Remove xhtml2pdf and python-docx from requirements.txt after transition.
Remove the old HTML report templates (reports/templates/reports/*.html).
Remove reports/sections/ Python modules (DOCX section builders — replaced by MD).
evidence: Added "md" to ReportHistory.FORMAT_CHOICES + migration (0003)
reports/templates/reports/report_template.md: full MD report template with all 7 sections
reports/utils.py: generate_report_file() supports format="md" (Django template renders MD)
ReportService.MIME_MAP: md → text/markdown
project_detail.html: Generate Markdown button added
report_list.html: MD badge (primary color) in format column
./init.sh passes
features.notes: Supersedes improve-003 and improve-004. Approach: generate report context dict → render via Django MD template → save .md file. PDF conversion can use pandoc, markdown-pdf, or weasyprint later. Keep ReportHistory model — just change format to "md". Remove xhtml2pdf dependency, remove python-docx dependency. Simplifies codebase significantly: no more dual-path maintenance, no HTML→PDF rendering issues, no DOCX layout bugs.

features.title: Custom user model replacing django.contrib.auth.models.User
features.user_visible_behavior: No visible change to end users. Future user profile fields (phone, department, avatar) can be added without migration pain.
verification: Run `python manage.py check` — no errors.
Create a new user via Django admin.
Verify user can log in.
Verify existing assignments still reference the correct user.
Verify project_detail shows team members correctly.
features.notes: This is a foundational refactor. Do it early before data grows. Use AbstractUser, add minimal extra fields (e.g. department, phone). Requires a fresh migration reset since no production data exists.

features.title: Default port 8001 — 8000 occupied by Docker Desktop on macOS
features.user_visible_behavior: runserver and docker compose always use port 8001. No more port-conflict errors.
verification: Run `python manage.py runserver` — starts on 8001 (not 8000).
Run `docker compose up` — app accessible on http://127.0.0.1:8001/.
Verify init.sh output mentions port 8001.
Verify no port-conflict errors.
evidence: AGENTS.md: port reference updated to 8001
init.sh: added runserver hint with port 8001
docker-compose.yml: already uses 8001:8000 mapping (unchanged)
entrypoint.sh: container-internal port stays 8000 — Docker maps host 8001 to container 8000 (unchanged)
./init.sh passes
features.notes: Port 8000 is always occupied by Docker Desktop on macOS. docker-compose.yml already uses 8001. Need to update: manage.py or settings.py RUNSERVER_PORT, init.sh banner, and any hardcoded URL references in documentation.

features.title: Report Preview (MD) — render Markdown as HTML in browser
features.user_visible_behavior: On the report list and project detail page, clicking Preview opens the Markdown report rendered as styled HTML in a new tab (like PDF preview, but for MD).
verification: Generate a report from a project.
Click Preview on a done MD report.
Verify the browser renders formatted HTML (headings, tables, bold, code blocks).
Verify report_list.html shows a Preview button for MD reports.
Verify project_detail.html shows Preview option.
./init.sh passes.
evidence: pip install markdown added to requirements.txt
reports/views.py: preview_report view — reads .md file, converts to HTML via markdown.markdown()
HTML wrapped in Bootstrap 4 styled page with headings, tables, code blocks, images
reports/urls.py: path("preview/<int:report_id>/") → preview_report
report_list.html: Preview button added for all done reports (replaces PDF-only preview)
project_detail.html: Preview button added for all done reports
Tested: MD→HTML conversion works (h1-h3, tables, bold, code, blockquotes)
./init.sh passes, 22 tests pass
features.notes: Use Python markdown library (pip install markdown) to convert .md content to HTML. Serve via Django view that reads the file, converts MD→HTML, wraps in a minimal HTML template with styling. Add markdown to requirements.txt.

features.title: Edit Template Design — admin UI for editing the MD report template
features.user_visible_behavior: Admins can edit the Markdown report template via a web UI (/reports/template/). Changes are saved to a database-backed template that overrides the default file-based template.
verification: Navigate to /reports/template/ as superuser.
Verify the current template content is displayed in a text editor.
Edit the template, save.
Generate a new report — verify it uses the edited template.

features.title: JS Dependencies v4 — TomSelect, flatpickr, Summernote BS5, IMask
features.user_visible_behavior: Select dropdowns, date pickers, rich text editor, and input masks all work with Bootstrap 5 without jQuery dependencies.
verification: Select2 replaced with TomSelect — select dropdowns render correctly.
Tempusdominus replaced with flatpickr — date inputs show date picker.
Summernote upgraded to BS5-compatible version — PoC editor renders correctly.
InputMask replaced with IMask (or removed if not used).
BS-Custom-File-Input removed (Bootstrap 5 native).
./init.sh + 22 tests pass.
features.notes: TomSelect: vanilla JS, BS5-compatible, drop-in CDN. flatpickr: lightweight date picker, no jQuery. Summernote: upgrade django-summernote to v0.10+ (Summernote 0.9+ has BS5 support) OR replace with django-quill-editor (Quill.js). IMask: vanilla JS input mask library.

features.title: Icons Migration v4 — Font Awesome → Bootstrap Icons (contextual mapping)
features.user_visible_behavior: All icons are replaced with semantic Bootstrap Icons — severity badges include colored icons, action buttons have contextual icons, navigation is intuitive.
verification: Verify no fa fa-* class remains in any template.
Verify sidebar icons use bi bi-* with semantic choices.
Verify severity badges show colored icons (Critical=diamond, High=triangle, etc).
Verify action buttons have contextual icons (edit=pencil, delete=trash, download=download, etc).
Verify status badges have icons (Open=folder, Closed=check-circle).
./init.sh + 22 tests pass.
features.notes: See the contextual icon mapping document. Key principles: right=forward, left=back, down=download, up=collapse. Color coding: Critical=text-danger + diamond, High=text-warning + triangle, Medium=text-info + info-circle, Low=text-success + arrow-down.
